title: Missile lodged in fuel tank: Navy's daring op to recover warhead from UAE-Kochi oil tanker
content: The Indian Navy successfully disposed of an unexploded missile warhead from a crude oil tanker, MT Olympic Life, which was en route to Kochi. The operation involved a specialist explosive ordnance disposal team that safely extracted the warhead lodged in a fuel tank, ensuring the safety of the ship and its crew.

title: March to dominance: How victories in states is consolidating BJP in Parliament
content: The BJP, once a two-seat party, now dominates Indian politics, transforming Atal Bihari Vajpayee's prophecy into reality. Recent state victories, particularly in West Bengal, are strategically bolstering its parliamentary numbers. This focus on consolidation aims to achieve long-term legislative goals like 'One Nation, One Election' and the Uniform Civil Code.

title: Situation Room with a view: Trump, Epstein and Washington’s 'wag the dog' whispers
content: A new book reveals a White House meeting in July 2025 where officials debated the political fallout from the Jeffrey Epstein files. Vice President JD Vance reportedly urged transparency, warning the issue wouldn't fade. Discussions even touched on releasing documents with allegations about Trump's alleged fixation with nipples, a proposal Vance believed Trump could overcome.

title: 'He is not just your T20 slogger': Manjrekar predicts big ODI future for Sooryavanshi
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is impressing beyond T20 cricket, showcasing adaptability in Sri Lanka's tri-series. Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar lauded his versatile 44 off 22 balls against Afghanistan A, highlighting his potential for longer formats. Suryavanshi's innings, marked by timing and placement, earned praise for his technique, not just aggression.

title: Rs 370 Biryani remark: Maharashtra police books comedian More and Jangra; summons Sejal Pawar
content: Maharashtra Cyber police have filed a case against comedian Pranit More, web developer Himanshu Jangra, and others for allegedly circulating obscene content. The controversy stems from remarks made during a comedy show that allegedly glorified coercion and trivialized consent, with further offensive comments made about medical cadavers. Summons have been issued for questioning.

title: Microsoft’s Satya Nadella says CEOs of some AI companies sent wrong message to Americans
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella believes some AI companies are eroding public trust by promoting negative narratives about job displacement while simultaneously celebrating AI development. He argues this contradictory messaging creates skepticism, and the industry must now focus on demonstrating tangible, practical benefits to rebuild confidence and gain public support.

title: Invade baby, invade: Trump eyes Kharg Island, Iran's energy spigot
content: President Trump is openly threatening to seize Iran's Kharg Island, a critical oil export hub, as a drastic measure to force Tehran into a deal. This marks a significant shift from his previous anti-interventionist stance, raising concerns about escalation and the potential for a costly, open-ended deployment.

title: Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
content: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: Palantir CEO’s message to tech CEO: Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: Delhi-born Nikhil Chaudhary earns maiden Australia call-up for Bangladesh T20Is
content: Delhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary has been named in Australia's T20I squad for the Bangladesh series, marking a significant milestone as the first Indian-born male cricketer in over 60 years to achieve this. His selection follows impressive performances in the Big Bash League, and he is now in contention for his international debut.

title: TN CM Vijay meets PM Modi; urges Centre to exempt state from NEET, release pending funds
content: Addressing the council, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Vijay said his govt would work constructively with the Union govt while firmly safeguarding TN’s interests and aspirations.

title: India retains growth edge with 6.6% growth, World Bank forecasts; cuts global outlook
content: Global growth forecasts are down to 2.5% for 2026, with the Middle East conflict and rising energy prices cited as key reasons. Despite this, India is set to remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, projected at 6.6% in fiscal year 2026-27. However, higher energy costs may moderate India's growth, while fiscal deficits are expected to widen across South Asia.

title: Hospital bills, captive patients: Why CCI’s ruling may weaken consumer protection
content: The Competition Commission of India has cleared twelve Delhi hospitals of overcharging patients. The CCI ruled that regulating healthcare prices is the government's responsibility, not the commission's. This decision comes after a decade-long investigation into allegations of inflated charges for medicines, consumables, and diagnostics. The ruling has sparked debate about consumer protection in India's vast healthcare sector.

title: 'Anything but brute force': Ashwin blown away by Sooryavanshi's 44-run blitz
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi impressed Ravichandran Ashwin with a dazzling 44 off 22 balls for India A. The 15-year-old showcased a blend of aggression and control, hitting nine boundaries. Despite missing a half-century, his performance highlighted his potential beyond T20s, earning praise from the former India spinner for his strokeplay and timing.

title: Anvay Dravid, son of legendary Rahul Dravid, named in India U-19 squad
content: Anvay Dravid, son of former India captain Rahul Dravid, has been selected for the India Under-19 squad touring Sri Lanka. The BCCI announced the squads on Thursday, with Anvay named as one of the wicketkeepers for the one-day matches. This selection follows his impressive performance in the Vinoo Mankad Trophy.

title: 10 days, a cremation and a cover-up: Woman’s final video exposes murder plot
content: A 24-year-old woman's death, initially reported as natural, has been revealed as a brutal murder. Police arrested her mother, husband, and father-in-law, who allegedly killed her and cremated the body to conceal the crime. The motive appears to stem from her desire to separate and marry another man.

title: Kerala on alert after suspected Nipah infection detected in Kozhikode, confirms health minister
content: In Kozhikode, health officials have confirmed a positive test for the Nipah virus in a patient currently requiring ventilator assistance due to severe fever. Authorities are actively tracing potential contacts, having identified 77 people, including healthcare staff and close family, who are under watch.

title: UK approves first weight loss pill as GLP-1 tablet gets green light from MHRA
content: The UK's MHRA has approved the first GLP-1 receptor agonist tablet for weight loss, offering a more convenient alternative to weekly injections. Semaglutide, branded as Wegovy, is now available in pill form for adults with obesity or those who are overweight with weight-related health conditions. Patients can transition directly from the weekly injection to the 25 mg daily tablet.

title: Veteran actor Dinyar Tirandaz passes away in Mumbai
content: Veteran actor Dinyar Tirandaz, known for his memorable roles in television shows and Bollywood films, has reportedly passed away in Mumbai. The news of his demise surfaced on social media on Thursday, leaving fans and members of the entertainment industry saddened.

title: Drunk Delhi cop enters Gurgaon society, assaults woman and threatens to rape her sister
content: A 21-year-old woman was allegedly assaulted by an intoxicated Delhi Police constable in Gurgaon's Sector 56. The accused also threatened her family and issued a rape threat to her younger sister. The constable was arrested and his bail application was dismissed. Police have added POCSO charges. CCTV footage is being examined.

title: Lalu's 79th birthday: RJD worker climbs JCB to cut cake, falls, but continues celebrating
content: During Lalu Prasad Yadav's birthday celebrations in Bihar's Vaishali district, RJD leader Kedar Yadav fell from a JCB machine while attempting to cut a cake. Despite the mishap, he continued the festivities, comparing Yadav to Nelson Mandela and calling him a leader of the poor.

title: SpaceX IPO tomorrow: Could Elon Musk become world's first trillionaire? Decoding the math
content: Elon Musk may soon become the world's first trillionaire. This is due to the upcoming initial public offering of his company SpaceX. The IPO is expected to be the largest in history. If valuations hold true, Musk's personal fortune could surge past the trillion-dollar mark. This event could reshape global wealth rankings and create new millionaires.

title: Meenakshi Natarajan row ends in BJP’s 3-0 Rajya Sabha sweep in MP amid Congress protest
content: The BJP secured three Rajya Sabha seats in Madhya Pradesh unopposed after the Congress nominee's nomination was rejected. Despite legal challenges and protests by the Congress, the Election Commission and Supreme Court did not intervene in time, leading to the BJP's victory. This outcome concluded a politically charged battle marked by allegations of horse-trading and constitutional conspiracy.

title: How promises of jobs and a better life trapped 453 Nepalese youths in UP
content: Hundreds of Nepalese youths, lured by job promises, were rescued from a sophisticated scam in Uttar Pradesh's Kushinagar.  Victims paid hefty sums for training and placements that never materialized, facing confinement and exploitation.  Alerts to the Nepal Embassy triggered a multi-agency operation, exposing the extensive cross-border racket.

title: 'Rs 370 Biryani' row: FIR against Pranit More, others for objectionable content online
content: Stand-up comedian Pranit More faces an FIR from Maharashtra Cyber Cell for allegedly disseminating obscene content. The case stems from an audience member's controversial date story shared during More's show, which sparked debate on accountability in comedy and content creation. Both More and the audience member have since apologized.

title: ‘Human shield’ on India border: Bangladesh opposition to launch protest over alleged 'push-ins'
content: Bangladesh's Jamaat-e-Islami and opposition parties are protesting alleged Indian "push-ins" and border killings. They claim thousands were pushed back and 19 Bangladeshis killed by Indian forces. Opposition leaders demand stronger border security and criticize India's stance, emphasizing the impact on Bangladeshi lives and sovereignty.

title: Greater Noida toll plaza chaos: Miscreants break barrier, threaten employees; videos go viral
content: Alleged toll dodgers caused chaos at Greater Noida's Luharli Toll Plaza, refusing to pay fees and breaking through a barrier.  The group reportedly confronted and threatened toll plaza employees before speeding away. Police are reviewing CCTV footage to identify and apprehend the individuals involved.

title: Buyer claims Rs 1.65 crore Defender failed its speed claim; court orders full refund
content: A luxury car buyer has won a major consumer dispute against Jaguar Land Rover India. The company was ordered to refund ₹1.65 crore with interest for a defective Defender SUV. The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance, had missing features, and underwent unauthorized chassis modifications, leading to the commission's strong ruling.

title: The capital's soldier-in-chief: What the Delhi area commander does
content: Lieutenant General Rajesh Sethi has taken command of Delhi Area, a crucial yet often overlooked Indian Army role. Beyond national ceremonies, he manages extensive administrative and operational duties in the capital. A decorated Kargil veteran, Sethi's tenure promises continued innovation in military traditions and robust support for the Army's presence in Delhi.

title: Afghanistan A shock India A despite fifties from Tilak, Ruturaj and Prabhsimran
content: Afghanistan A secured a surprising four-run victory over India A in a rain-affected tri-series match, successfully chasing a revised target of 294 in 38 overs. Despite India A posting a strong 349/9, Afghanistan A stayed ahead of the DLS par score when play was halted, clinching a memorable win.

title: 'Choose Abhishek or me': Kalyan Banerjee's blunt message to Mamata amid turmoil in TMC
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee issued an ultimatum to Mamata Banerjee, demanding she choose between her nephew Abhishek Banerjee and senior party workers. Banerjee also announced he would no longer represent Abhishek in legal matters, citing disrespect and arrogance from the latter. This comes amid internal party tensions following electoral setbacks.

title: 'Rs 370 biryani' remark: NCW summons comedian Pranit More, web developer Himanshu Jangra
content: The National Commission for Women is investigating a stand-up comedy show in Gurugram. A viral video showed remarks allegedly glorifying sexual coercion. The NCW has asked the Haryana Police for an action report within seven days. Comedians Pranit More and Himanshu Jangra have been summoned. The NCW stressed that consent is non-negotiable.

title: Tom Holland’s ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ to release early in India: All you need to know
content: Indian fans of Tom Holland's 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day' have reason to cheer as the film's release has been advanced by a day to July 30. Pre-sale tickets for premium formats like IMAX will be available from June 17. The highly anticipated movie picks up four years after 'No Way Home,' showcasing a more grounded Spider-Man facing new, mysterious threats.

title: 'Will not leave Mamata in difficult time': Shatrughan Sinha picks Team Didi amid TMC turmoil
content: Amidst a party exodus, TMC MP Shatrughan Sinha pledged loyalty to Mamata Banerjee, stating he wouldn't abandon her during challenging times, recalling her support for him. This declaration follows reports of his alleged involvement in forming a separate faction. Earlier, Sinha also congratulated Prime Minister Narendra Modi on his 12 years in office, calling him a "friend and guide."

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity benchmarks closed lower on Thursday, impacted by global weakness, rising crude oil, and Middle East tensions. IT stocks saw significant selling, while banking and pharma offered some support. Markets experienced volatility throughout the session, reflecting investor caution amid geopolitical concerns and FII outflows.

title: 'Inappropriate to abandon a ship in troubled waters': Babul Supriyo on leaving TMC
content: TMC leaders Babul Supriyo and Shatrughan Sinha have reaffirmed their loyalty to Mamata Banerjee amidst a party exodus. Both stated they would not abandon the party during its troubled times, emphasizing Banerjee's past support for them. This comes as the TMC faces a significant crisis following electoral defeat and a rebellion weakening its legislative strength.

title: 'All soldiers of Congress': Pilot plays down Gehlot's remarks, says he has always shown affection
content: Sachin Pilot downplayed Ashok Gehlot's recent criticism, emphasizing party unity and affection. Pilot highlighted national challenges like exam paper leaks and rising prices, vowing to strengthen the INDIA bloc against the BJP. He also criticized the Rajasthan government and alleged institutional pressure on the Election Commission.

title: ‘Pushpa of Falta’, TMC leader Jahangir Khan paraded in shorts after arrest
content: Trinamool Congress leader Jahangir Khan, also known as ‘Pushpa’, was paraded through Falta by police following his arrest near the India-Nepal border. Khan, who unsuccessfully contested the Falta assembly seat, was remanded to five days police custody in connection with seven extortion and other charges.

title: 'By next World Cup, India must have a backup': Raina on Pandya injuries
content: Suresh Raina has urged India to find a dependable backup for all-rounder Hardik Pandya, citing his persistent fitness issues. Pandya's recent injury, ruling him out of the Afghanistan series, underscores the team's need for depth in pace-bowling all-rounders ahead of the next ODI World Cup. Identifying and nurturing an alternative is crucial to avoid vulnerability.

title: Precision and dominance: Navy unveils crest of Project 17A frigate Dunagiri
content: India's INS Dunagiri, a new stealth frigate, boasts advanced multi-domain combat capabilities. Named after a Himalayan peak, it features BrahMos and Barak-8 missiles for formidable offensive and defensive power. This advanced warship, built by GRSE, signifies a major leap in indigenous naval technology and is set for commissioning soon.

title: Wipro Rs 15,000 crore buyback opens - what it means for retail investors
content: Wipro's Rs 15,000 crore share buyback is now open, offering shareholders Rs 250 per share, a significant premium over the current market price. The offer, valid until June 17, presents a potential arbitrage opportunity for retail investors. However, analysts suggest limited gains, with acceptance ratios expected around 20%, and caution about risks associated with unaccepted shares.

title: NTR District Collector urges public to send their wards to govt. schools
content: With modern teaching methods, improved infrastructure, and skill-development programmes, the government schools are being strengthened to compete with their private counterparts, says Collector

title: Kudumbashree district mission pilots ‘Kunjaadu’ in Vembayam for animal husbandry promotion
content: An increase in number of beneficiaries under the initiative is expected to lead to a gradual rise in livestock population

title: NC and BJP condemn Pakistan’s ‘atrocities in PoK’, seek UN intervention
content: NC chief Farooq Abdullah appealed to UNHRC to investigate the incidents and show the truth to the world of actions by Pakistan in PoK

title: Calcutta HC questions expelled TMC MLA Ritabrata Banerjee’s appointment as LoP by Speaker
content: Appearing for petitioners, senior advocate and TMC MP Kalyan Bandopadhyay argues that the Speaker’s decision ‘hit the Constitution’s basic structure’ by violating its tenth schedule that settled law governing relationship between a political party and its legislature party

title: Addressing drinking water, sewage, power issues key to Thiruvananthapuram’s growth, says Minister C.P. John
content: Transport Minister says there is a substantial gap between the demand for and supply of drinking water in the city; he adds that capacity of power substations should be upgraded to meet growing demands

title: Monsoon 2026 tracker LIVE: India expected to receive below-average rain over next two weeks, report says
content: The IMD has forecast light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms over parts of Delhi later in the night

title: The apex court rings its own chain
content: The Supreme Court’s reliance on suo motu cognisance has turned a once ‘rare but highly visible’ jurisdiction into a recurring instrument shaped by primetime attention and media reports; while it keeps ‘ringing’ its own chain through televised listings and supervision, the trial courts below it continue doing the work

title: India expected to be among the countries invited to join initiative to secure Strait of Hormuz: French sources
content: All issues including West Asia will be discussed when PM Modi meets President Macron, says MEA's Secretary (West) Sibi George

title: Tamil Nadu is committed to working constructively with Centre, says Vijay
content: Tamil Nadu wholeheartedly supports the nation’s aspirations and believes a developed India can be built only through empowered States, cooperative federalism, and inclusive development, the Chief Minister says in his address at the 11th Governing Council meeting of NITI Aayog

title: Congress calls rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination ‘seat chori’
content: It says it will fight the rejection of candidate’s papers legally and politically; party plans nation-wide agitation on inflation, unemployment, NEET paper leak, CBSE OSM row among others issues

title: Kalyan Banerjee calls Abhishek ‘arrogant’; gives ultimatum to Mamata
content: The senior leader asks the Trinamool supremo to choose between him and Abhishek Banerjee amid escalating rebellion in the party

title: IIT-K hires ethical hacker who flagged ‘vulnerabilities’ in CBSE portal
content: To encourage bright students Nisarga Adhikary, IIT-K is launching a Bachelors in Cybersecurity where the admission will not be through JEE (Advanced) but through a hackathon, says institute director Manindra Agarwal

title: Kerala HC orders CBI to take over probe into 23 child deaths in Palakkad
content: Court seeks CBI’s response and directs the agency to obtain required clarifications from the District Police Chief and the Kerala Police

title: Watch: Government waives excise duty on E22 to E30 petrol blends
content: India has removed excise duty on petrol blended with 22%–30% ethanol, giving a boost to higher biofuel blends. What does this mean for fuel prices, farmers, flex-fuel vehicles and India’s energy security? Watch our explainer.

title: ‘It is only through Bharathiraja’s films that we can see what the villages of Tamil Nadu were once like’
content: “It was Bharathiraja who, for the first time, brought the villages of southern Tamil Nadu — especially those around Madurai — alive before our eyes”

title: One year since AI-171 crash, pilots body questions delay in releasing final probe report
content: The Federation of Indian Pilots urges authorities not to publish an incomplete interim report, warning that it could add to speculation on the cause of the June 2025 crash. Officials say engine manufacturer General Electric is still analysing data from AI-171 engines, contributing to the delay.

title: CM must reveal those behind Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination rejection: Harish Rao
content: He alleges huge scam in education department, cites it as the reason for delay in reopening of schools

title: Kerala HC seeks report on steps to declog canals, drains, and ensure safe roads in Kochi
content: Collector-led panel asked to submit a report by June 18; report to detail the progress of works and preventive measures being taken

title: Kerala reports 34 cases of shigellosis in June so far
content: Three more children test positive for the infection at Wayanad Mar Baselios HSS on June 11, where around 514 children have shown symptoms

title: Watch: Three Indian seafarers killed off Oman coast| Above the Fold| 11.06.2026
content: From the deaths of three Indian seafarers in a U.S. strike near Oman to Kerala’s Nipah virus alert and the Cockroach Janta Party’s education manifesto - Top stories of the day.

title: Psychological evaluation of children in custody disputes must involve ‘minimum intrusion’, says Supreme Court
content: Cautioning Family Courts against routinely directing psychological evaluations of children, Bench says the welfare, emotional security, dignity and psychological well-being of the child must remain paramount consideration in all proceedings

title: A.P. govt. to issue DSC notification in October, exams in December this year
content: The announcement is in accordance with the job calendar, says Education Secretary; DSC-2025 recruitment process has been conducted with complete transparency and in strict accordance with established rules, he adds, refuting allegations of irregularities

title: Containment steps in Kozhikode after Ramanattukara man tests positive for Nipah
content: Seventy-seven persons on contact list, 15 people quarantined. Health department working in coordination with departments of Forest and Wildlife and Animal Husbandry

title: BASE Economics student from Bengaluru selected to represent India for Youth Ambassador Programme 2026
content: Youth Ambassador Programme is one the largest youth led literacy campaigns organised by the World Literacy Foundation located in Australia. It aims to empower young people aged 16-25 to advocate for literacy in their own communities

title: West Bengal MLAs signature forgery case: Abhishek Banerjee appears before CID
content: The HC grants interim protection to Abhishek Banerjee, directing that the CID cannot take any coercive action as long as he cooperates with the investigation

title: Renewable energy producers look for policy changes from T.N. government
content: A solar energy project goes through seven stages, from land acquisition to commissioning, and takes about three months; the process should be made seamless, say investors

title: Despite monsoon setting in, heatwave conditions to persist in Telangana till weekend
content: The highest temperature on Wednesday (June 10, 2026) was 43°C and 18 districts recorded maximum temperature exceeding 42°C. Thunderstorms are predicted in 18 districts for Friday.

title: Coalition flags concerns over lake works undertaken with World Bank funds
content: It flags three concerns: concrete-heavy engineering, hydrological discontinuity and buffer zone encroachment.

title: Student suicides: National Task Force says there is no ‘foolproof’ way to prevent them, but calls for overhaul of campus support systems
content: Seeks standardised mental health services, timely scholarships, faculty recruitment and better data collection

title: Central, coastal A.P. receives season’s first spell of heavy rainfall
content: NTR district records widespread and very heavy rainfall during the 24 hours ending Thursday morning, while the neighbouring districts of Krishna, Guntur, Bapatla also receives widespread heavy rainfall

title: Revanth urges Modi to facilitate meeting with Fadnavis, approvals for PRLIS
content: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy called on Prime Minister Narendra Modi as part of his New Delhi visit

title: Police flag possibility of cyber fraudsters using fake TV apps during FIFA World Cup
content: Kerala Police advise public not to install APK files received through social media for watching FIFA World Cup matches

title: 18 python hatchlings found on house premises in Thrissur
content: Family, in coordination with forest officials, is preparing to break open parts of the compound wall for further inspection

title: Thiruvananthapuram Corporation launches campaign to prevent monsoon-related diseases
content: The civic body will strengthen preventive measures in the coming days, including awareness campaigns and vector-control activities

title: Omar Abdullah Doubles Down On J&K Statehood Demand, Meets PM Modi
content: In a statement, the Chief Minister's Office said that Omar Abdullah also highlighted the need for continued support from the Government of India.

title: Dreaded Gangster Vainket Garg Extradited To India From Georgia
content: Garg, a known gangster, was wanted in several cases registered by the Haryana Police for offences relating to murder, attempt to murder, extortion, organised crime and use of illegal firearms, according to the CBI.

title: Ship Manager Blames US For Deaths Of 3 Indians, Denies Iran Oil Link
content: The vessel manager argued that the circumstances surrounding the attack have not been adequately explained and that the families of those who died deserve clear answers regarding what occurred.

title: At Ahmedabad College Where Air India Plane Crashed, Time Still Stopped A Year On
content: Twelve months after the aviation accident, NDTV returned to the crash site where memories of that horrific day continue to haunt survivors, families and an entire community

title: After AI-171, Air India Focuses On Flight Safety, Mental Health
content: A network of 265 psychologists is providing large-scale, accessible mental health support, complemented by confidential therapy and psychiatry access for employees and their families

title: Violence Continues in Manipur, 2 Dead In Pre-Dawn Attack In Kamjong
content: Clashes were reported between security personnel and angry mourners, including family members and residents who had gathered to pay their last respects.

title: Case Registered Against Comedian, Man Behind Rs 370 Ki Biryani' Row
content: The videos contained offensive remarks concerning women, consent, and deceased persons that violate public decency, he said.

title: Israel Plans Relocation Of Bnei Menashe Community From India Within 4 Years
content: Speaking at a ceremony in the northern Israeli city of Nof Hagalil honouring newly arrived immigrants, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the government intends to facilitate the relocation of all remaining members of the community.

title: Used Cars, New Loans: What's Behind India's Auto Credit Rise
content: Car buyers have become aspirational and vehicle loans are witnessing robust growth. The twist is that the used car market is going premium and attracting buyers even with a vehicle loan.

title: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy Seeks PM's Support For Mega Projects
content: The Chief Minister pressed for approval of Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase-II, a Rs 38,595-crore project covering 122.9 km across seven corridors.

title: "Seeks To Create Democratic Deficit": BJP On Rahul Gandhi's PM Remarks
content: The BJP accused the Leader of opposition of being jealous of the Prime Minister's success and called him a "political anarchist".

title: Indian Sailors Killed In Attack Near Strait Of Hormuz, Spark Safety Concerns
content: In a detailed press statement issued on Thursday, the union expressed "serious concern" over what it described as a worsening security situation for merchant vessels transiting one of the world's most strategically important maritime corridors.

title: Air India To Observe 2-Minute Silence Tomorrow For AI171 Crash Victims
content: The observance comes as the investigation into the June 12, 2025, AI171 disaster, one of India's deadliest aviation accidents, remains underway.

title: Inside Indian Navy's High-Risk Mission To Remove Missile Warhead From Tanker
content: Considering the risks of handling an unexploded warhead inside a fuel storage area, the Navy team carried out the operation in a carefully planned manner to ensure the safety of the vessel, its crew and nearby port facilities.

title: Why Iran War May Push India To Double Fertiliser Subsidy
content: India is one of the world's largest importers of DAP fertiliser and also relies heavily on imported LNG, which is used to produce urea.

title: Tourism Secretary Flags Overcrowding At Key Hotspots
content: India's tourism sector has witnessed unprecedented growth over the past decade, driven by massive investments in infrastructure and rising domestic travel.

title: Delhi Cop Assaults Woman In Gurugram, Runs Thar Into Apartment Complex Gate
content: Ronit Yadav, the accused, also attempted to barge into the house of the victim and threatened her mother and minor sister. He later rammed the society's gate with his Thar SUV.

title: Naked Man, 58, Assaulted By Mob For Peeping Into Woman's Bedroom In Kerala
content: Varghese Mathew is currently undergoing treatment at Kottayam Medical College Hospital. Police said he informed investigators that he does not wish to pursue a complaint regarding the assault.
